encounter.type: subspecialty office visit, pediatric allergy and immunology
encounter.reason: asthma reassessment, fractional exhaled nitric oxide testing, spirometry attempted
encounter.location: UNC Medical Center, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis
tree nut allergy — cashew, almond, walnut

medications.name: fluticasone/salmeterol (Advair HFA) 45/21 mcg inhaler
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer

medications.name: albuterol (ProAir HFA) 90 mcg inhaler
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath via spacer

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr) 0.15 mg
medications.sig: as needed for anaphylaxis, carried at all times
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: apply to eczema flares twice daily as needed

document_references.description: Subspecialty visit note — pediatric allergy/immunology office visit note documenting asthma reassessment at 2 months post-therapy step-up, FeNO result showing ongoing eosinophilic inflammation, technically inadequate spirometry, physical exam findings, review of all atopic conditions, and plan to continue current regimen with environmental control reinforcement

FeNO test result report — point-of-care fractional exhaled nitric oxide measurement result: 38 ppb (elevated for age), consistent with eosinophilic airway inflammation despite current medium-dose ICS/LABA plus LTRA therapy

Spirometry report (technically inadequate) — documentation of spirometry attempt that was not interpretable due to patient's inability to perform reproducible forced expiratory maneuvers across 6 attempts; noted as age-related limitation, to be reattempted at future visit

Environmental control handout (patient education) — patient education materials on environmental control measures for asthma management, including HEPA air filtration, dust mite encasings, and trigger avoidance strategies

diagnostic_reports.description: Spirometry was attempted but was technically inadequate. Patient (age 4 years 2 months) was unable to perform a reproducible forced expiratory maneuver. Could not sustain exhalation for minimum acceptable duration. Flow-volume loops were not reproducible across 6 attempts. Test documented as not interpretable. Will be reattempted at next visit when patient is older and more cooperative.
